goal: Earn 2% cashback on everyday in-person purchases using the HSBC Global Debit Card
summary: The HSBC Global Everyday Debit Card offers 2% cashback on in-person purchases under $100, with no annual fee. Used consistently for daily spending, community members report saving $10–20/month with typical spend levels, rising to ~$50/month at the $2,500/month eligible-spend cap.

steps.action: Apply for the HSBC Global Everyday Account and Debit Card
steps.detail: The account is free to open online. There is no annual fee on the debit card. Note the card cannot be used for online transactions — it is designed for in-person purchases only.

steps.action: Use the card for in-person tap-and-go purchases under $100
steps.detail: 2% cashback applies to in-store purchases under $100 per transaction. Google Pay with the HSBC debit card may also qualify. For purchases over $100, split into multiple sub-$100 transactions where practical to stay within the cashback tier.

steps.action: Direct everyday spending (transport, groceries, cafés) through this card
steps.detail: Community discussion notes the card may work for public transport tap-on/tap-off (e.g. Opal) and in-store supermarket purchases — verify these work for your use case before relying on them. The OP confirmed saving $10–20/month at their spend level.

steps.action: Track monthly eligible spend to approach the $2,500/month cap
steps.detail: The maximum cashback benefit is achieved at $2,500/month of eligible in-person spend (= $50/month cashback). Spend above the cap does not earn further cashback. Always verify current HSBC product terms as rates and conditions may change.

total_est_saving: ~$120–600/year depending on eligible in-person spend volume
risks: Card does not work for online transactions — a separate card is still needed for e-commerce
2% cashback applies per transaction only to purchases under $100; plan accordingly for larger in-store purchases
Public transport tap-on/tap-off and supermarket in-store eligibility should be verified against current HSBC merchant category terms
Cashback rate and monthly cap are subject to HSBC product changes
prerequisites: Australian resident with valid ID for HSBC KYC
Primary day-to-day spending mix must include a meaningful share of in-person sub-$100 purchases
